title: The Tailor Shop
introKo:
결혼식 하객 정장을 맞추러 간 양복점, 유난히 꼼꼼한 재단사 Mr. Pin의 이야기.
…표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

quiz.q: Mr. Pin이 무릎까지 꿇고 잰 것은요?

normal.text: Your best friend is getting married next month. You need a suit, so you visit a small tailor shop on a quiet street.
The tailor, Mr. Pin, welcomes you with a warm smile. People say no one in town measures as carefully as he does.
"A wedding suit must fit like a second skin," he says. "Please step up. First, I need to learn your measurements — all of them."

options.en: Sure. It's my first time getting a suit made.
options.reaction: "A first time is special," he says. "Relax and leave everything to me."

options.en: Okay. How long will this take?
options.reaction: "Good work takes time," he smiles. "I never rush the important parts."

normal.text: He wraps his tape measure around your chest, then your waist. "Breathe out slowly," he says. "Don't hold anything back."
"Nice and snug," he murmurs, pulling the tape a little closer. "The tape never lies. Your numbers are very good."

options.en: Is it supposed to be this tight?
options.reaction: "Snug is not tight," he says. "A good suit should hold you close all day."

options.en: You are very thorough, Mr. Pin.
options.reaction: "Always," he says. "I take my time with every inch."

normal.text: "Now, the inseam," he says, and he kneels in front of you. "Stand still. This part needs a steady hand."
He runs the tape up along the inside of your leg. "Almost done," he says. "Numbers must be honest."
Then he drapes soft fabric over your shoulders and pins it, piece by piece. "Hold still," he says. "Feel how it flows over the body."

options.reaction: "It should. Good fabric touches everything and hides nothing."

options.en: Will it be ready before the wedding?
options.reaction: "Of course. But come back for a second fitting. The second time is always better than the first."

normal.text: At last, he steps back and looks at you in the mirror. "You see?" he says. "By now, this suit knows your body better than you do."
On the wedding day, everyone asks where you got your suit. You just smile. Some things stay between you and your tailor.

hard.text: Your best friend is getting married next month, which means you finally need a proper suit. You find a small tailor shop tucked away on a quiet street.
The tailor, Mr. Pin, welcomes you with a knowing smile. People around town swear that nobody takes measurements as carefully as he does.
"A wedding suit must fit like a second skin," he declares. "Step up onto the platform, please. Before anything else, I must learn your measurements — every last one."

options.en: Sure. It's my first time having a suit made.
options.reaction: "A first time is always special," he says softly. "Relax, and leave everything in my hands."

options.en: Okay. Roughly how long will this take?
options.reaction: "Good work cannot be rushed," he smiles. "And I never hurry over the important parts."

hard.text: He wraps the tape measure around your chest, then lets it slide down to your waist. "Breathe out slowly," he says. "Don't hold anything back from me."
"Nice and snug," he murmurs, drawing the tape a touch closer. "The tape never lies, and I must say, your numbers are excellent."

options.en: Is it supposed to feel this tight?
options.reaction: "Snug is not the same as tight," he explains. "A proper suit should hold you close from morning until night."

options.en: You are remarkably thorough, Mr. Pin.
options.reaction: "Always," he says. "I always take my time with every single inch."

hard.text: "Now for the inseam," he says, kneeling down in front of you. "Stand perfectly still. This particular measurement calls for a steady hand."
He runs the tape up along the inside of your leg. "Almost there," he says. "An honest number is worth a little patience."
Then he drapes soft fabric across your shoulders, pinning it into place piece by piece. "Hold still," he warns. "Feel how it flows over the body — that is how you judge good fabric."

options.reaction: "It should. Fine fabric touches everything and hides nothing."

options.en: Will it be finished before the wedding?
options.reaction: "Naturally. But you must return for a second fitting — the second time is always better than the first."

hard.text: At last he steps back and looks at you in the mirror. "You see?" he says with quiet pride. "By the end, the suit knows your body better than you know it yourself."
On the wedding day, everyone wants to know where you found such a suit. You simply smile. Some things stay between you and your tailor.

pin.ko: 핀, 시침핀; 핀으로 고정하다 (여기서는 재단사 이름)

tucked.ko: (tucked away) 안쪽에 자리 잡은, 숨어 있는

rushed.ko: 재촉된, 급히 이뤄진 (rush의 과거분사; cannot be rushed: 서두른다고 되는 게 아니다)

wink.inseam: 안쪽 다리 길이. 재단사가 재는 부위 중 가장 민망한 곳이라, 어른들 농담에 단골로 등장해요.
wink.snug: 몸에 딱 맞아 아늑한. 옷 얘기지만, 두 사람의 거리가 지나치게 가까울 때도 이렇게 말해요.
wink.fit: 몸에 꼭 맞다. 옷이 아니라 두 사람이 잘 맞는다는 뜻으로 들릴 수도 있어요.
wink.pin: 핀으로 고정하다. 상대를 움직이지 못하게 붙잡아 둔다는 뜻도 있어서, 재단사 이름치고는 꽤 의미심장해요.
wink.measure: 치수를 재다. 온몸 구석구석 손이 가는 일이라, 이 가게에서는 줄자가 재는 게 치수만이 아닌 것처럼 들려요.
wink.kneels: 무릎을 꿇다. 치수 잴 때 재단사의 정석 자세지만, 다른 이야기에서는 청혼처럼 아주 간절한 순간에 나오는 자세이기도 해요.
wink.kneeling: 무릎을 꿇다. 치수 잴 때 재단사의 정석 자세지만, 다른 이야기에서는 청혼처럼 아주 간절한 순간에 나오는 자세이기도 해요.
wink.tape: 줄자. 몸을 감고 살짝 조이는 물건이라, 행간에서는 손길처럼 읽히기도 해요.
wink.flows: 흐르다. 원단이 몸을 따라 흐른다는 표현은, 무언가가 피부 위를 스쳐 가는 장면을 떠올리게 해요.
wink.second: 두 번째. 두 번째 가봉이 처음보다 낫다는 말이지만, 원어민 귀에는 다른 종류의 '두 번째'부터 떠올라요.

introEn:
A suit fitting with Mr. Pin, a remarkably thorough tailor.
...on the surface. Tap words to see the hidden meaning.
